audit_requirement: Attempt to invalidate every preserved pathway.

eliminated_pathway_audits.attempted_revival: The 1975 prototype might show Kodak buried the future.
eliminated_pathway_audits.pathway: P8: Technology blindness or invention suppression
eliminated_pathway_audits.reason: The standalone story is contradicted by extensive digital investment, technical leadership, and market participation.

preserved_pathway_audits.attempted_invalidation: Kodak was digitally capable and had market participation, so disruption may be too generic.
preserved_pathway_audits.pathway: P1: Digital disruption and ecosystem substitution
preserved_pathway_audits.reason: The pathway is not technology ignorance; it is market and ecosystem substitution documented by segment decline, camera exit, and smartphone/printing effects.
preserved_pathway_audits.residual_risk: Can over-dominate if treated as the whole answer.

preserved_pathway_audits.attempted_invalidation: Kodak made digital investments, so the business model was not fully rigid.
preserved_pathway_audits.pathway: P2: Business model rigidity and film-profit lock-in
preserved_pathway_audits.reason: The preserved claim is not total rigidity; it is failure to replace film/print economics with a viable recurring-margin model.

preserved_pathway_audits.attempted_invalidation: Management failure is a vague blame bucket and is weakened by evidence of serious renewal attempts.
preserved_pathway_audits.pathway: P3: Strategic management and execution failure
preserved_pathway_audits.reason: Preserved only as sequencing, separation, funding, and scaling failure across attempted renewal paths; not as simple ignorance.

preserved_pathway_audits.attempted_invalidation: The corpus lacks direct compensation-plan evidence.
preserved_pathway_audits.reason: Direct bonus evidence is absent, but resource-allocation incentives are supported by film-profit comparison, investment losses, analyst pressure, and budgeting/value-process evidence.
preserved_pathway_audits.residual_risk: Evidence is partly inferential rather than a direct internal-pay record.

preserved_pathway_audits.attempted_invalidation: Kodak's digital R&D and products contradict a culture-resistance story.
preserved_pathway_audits.pathway: P5: Organizational culture and middle-management cognition
preserved_pathway_audits.reason: The preserved claim is bounded: culture shaped interpretation and speed; it did not block all digital action.
preserved_pathway_audits.residual_risk: Weakened; should not be promoted above market and business-model pathways without more internal evidence.

preserved_pathway_audits.attempted_invalidation: Timing can become an excuse that removes agency.
preserved_pathway_audits.pathway: P6: Timing failures and market-uncertainty trap
preserved_pathway_audits.reason: Timing remains causal because early investment losses, delayed demand, late price competition, and smartphone substitution changed which strategies could pay off.
preserved_pathway_audits.residual_risk: Needs to be paired with management and capital paths.

preserved_pathway_audits.attempted_invalidation: Liquidity problems may be downstream symptoms.
preserved_pathway_audits.pathway: P7: Capital allocation and liquidity constraint
preserved_pathway_audits.reason: The pathway survives because capital pressure also narrowed later options: debt, divestitures, cash shortfalls, patent monetization, and Chapter 11 processes changed feasible moves.
preserved_pathway_audits.residual_risk: Causal direction is mixed and should remain secondary.
